seo.title: Building Content Generation with LlamaIndex + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with LlamaIndex + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: LlamaIndex enables powerful content generation by connecting LLMs to your indexed knowledge bases, allowing dynamic creation of blog posts, marketing copy, and more. However, without guardrails, outputs can drift from brand voice, hallucinate facts, or violate editorial guidelines. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure generated content meets brand consistency, factual accuracy, and compliance standards.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with LlamaIndex
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture starts with data ingestion where documents (brand guidelines, past content, fact sheets) are chunked and indexed using LlamaIndex's VectorStoreIndex or other retrievers. A query engine is built on this index, incorporating a custom prompt template that enforces brand voice, tone, and SEO keywords. During generation, a user query (e.g., 'Write a blog post about AI ethics') triggers retrieval of relevant context, which is synthesized by an LLM like GPT-4 into polished content.

Key components include the index for RAG, a synthesizer for response generation, and post-processing for formatting. This setup supports iterative refinement but is prone to context irrelevance or hallucinations if retrieval fails. Vex wraps the query engine to verify outputs against brand and factual criteria before delivery.

Scalability comes from persistent indexes and async querying, with monitoring for staleness via periodic re-indexing.
architectureOverview.diagram:
┌─────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐
│   User Query    │───▶│  LlamaIndex      │───▶│   LLM Response   │
│ (Blog Topic)    │    │ Query Engine     │    │ (Generated Copy) │
└─────────────────┘    │ + Vector Index   │    └──────────────────┘
                       │ + Brand Prompt   │           ▲
                       └──────────────────┘           │
                       ┌──────────────────┐           │
                       │   Documents      │◀──────────┘
                       │ (Brand Guide,    │
                       │  Facts, Style)   │
                       └──────────────────┘

Vex Verification Layer wraps Query Engine output

risks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
risks.description: Index retrieves brand guidelines when query is about product features, leading to off-topic content.
risks.mitigation: Use hybrid retrieval (semantic + keyword) and Vex to validate context-query alignment before synthesis.

risks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
risks.description: LLM invents statistics or brand claims not present in retrieved context.
risks.mitigation: Implement Vex fact-checking against source docs and enforce strict grounding prompts.

risks.name: Index staleness causing outdated responses
risks.description: Old product info in index leads to incorrect specs in generated content.
risks.mitigation: Schedule automated re-indexing and use Vex timestamp checks for freshness.

risks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
risks.description: Key brand voice instructions split across chunks, causing tone drift.
risks.mitigation: Use overlap chunking and Vex tone adherence scoring on full output.

implementation.title: Set up LlamaIndex with Brand Documents
implementation.description: Index your brand guidelines, style guide, and fact sheets for retrieval during content generation.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader, Settings
from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI

# Configure LLM
Settings.llm = OpenAI(model="gpt-4", temperature=0.1)

# Load brand documents
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader("./brand_docs").load_data()

# Create persistent index
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
index.storage_context.persist(persist_dir="./brand_index")

implementation.title: Build Content Generation Query Engine
implementation.description: Create a query engine with custom prompt enforcing brand voice and SEO.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import PromptTemplate
from llama_index.core.query_engine import CitationQueryEngine

brand_prompt = PromptTemplate(
    "Context: {context_str}\n\n"
    "Query: {query_str}\n\n"
    "Write engaging content in our brand voice: professional yet approachable. "
    "Include SEO keywords: {seo_keywords}. Ensure factual accuracy."
)

query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    similarity_top_k=5,
    node_postprocessors=[ ],  # Add custom
    text_qa_template=brand_prompt
)

implementation.title: TypeScript LlamaIndex Node Setup
implementation.description: Initialize LlamaIndex in Node.js for server-side content generation.

implementation.title: Generate Content with SEO Compliance
implementation.description: Query the engine for blog post generation with enforced guidelines.
implementation.code:
response = query_engine.query(
    "Write a 500-word blog post about sustainable AI practices. "
    "SEO keywords: green AI, ethical computing, carbon footprint."
)

print(response.response)

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Query Engine with Vex Verify (Python)
vexIntegration.description: Integrate Vex to verify brand consistency and factual accuracy on generated content.

vexIntegration.title: Vex Verification for TypeScript
vexIntegration.description: Use Vex SDK in Node.js to guard content generation outputs.

vexIntegration.title: Async Batch Content Generation with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Generate multiple pieces with parallel Vex verification for production scale.
vexIntegration.code:
import asyncio
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor

async def batch_generate(topics: list):
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    with ThreadPoolExecutor() as executor:
        tasks = [loop.run_in_executor(executor, generate_content, topic, ["AI", "tech"]) 
                for topic in topics]
        return await asyncio.gather(*tasks)

# Generates verified content for ['Topic1', 'Topic2']

cta.heading: Secure Your LlamaIndex Content Generation
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your query engine to eliminate content risks.
